title: Cape Cod’s Sundae School to offer 76-cent ice cream scoops
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sundae School to offer 76-cent ice cream scoops on June 14 from 2-4 p.m. at Harwich Port and Dennisport.
The 76-cent price reflects the year 1976 when Sundae School first opened in Cape Cod.
The anniversary celebration will occur at the Harwich Port location, 606 Main St., as part of Sundae School's 50-year milestone.

title: July 4th fireworks cancellation sparks intense pushback in Rutland
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rutland, Massachusetts, canceled its July 4th fireworks, parade, and concert due to insufficient public safety personnel.
Residents are upset that their donations for the events will not be refunded; funds were already allocated for expenses.
Massachusetts Fiscal Alliance criticized the decision and urged the Attorney General to investigate the handling of event funds.

title: 2 teen girls arrested after Boston police allegedly find multiple firearms, drugs, and a knife in vehicle
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police stopped a vehicle in Dorchester, recovering two loaded firearms, drugs, and a knife early Tuesday.
Jacqueline Rose Botelho, 18, and a 16-year-old from Taunton were arrested and face multiple firearm and drug charges.
Botelho pleaded not guilty and is held at Suffolk County Jail; her next court date is June 22.

title: T pushing new PSA after fatal escalator incident
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA to launch PSA campaign to highlight escalator emergency stop button usage after fatal incident at Davis Station.
Steven McCluskey died after being trapped in a Davis Station escalator; family and safety officials express concern.
MBTA General Manager Phil Eng emphasizes commitment to rider safety and potential surveillance upgrades after the incident.

title: A 3BR cabin-style home near the Berkshires — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 3-bedroom cabin-style home on 14.6 acres near the Berkshires is available for purchase.
Built in 1977, the 2,185 square-foot home features 3 baths, 1 half-bath, and private sewer/water.
The 2025 property taxes for the home are $6,396, and potential buyers are prompted to guess the listing price.

title: Mass. photographer stabbed in ‘unprovoked attack’ while shooting wedding in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Donald Halsing, 26, a wedding photographer from Mendon, Mass., was stabbed during a wedding reception at Kingsley Camp Ground, Maine.
Halsing suffered serious injuries requiring emergency surgery after an 'unprovoked attack' and is expected to have a long recovery.
Andrew Manderson, 26, a wedding guest, was arrested and charged with elevated aggravated assault; he's held in Cumberland County Jail.

title: Conan O’Brien inspires grads, rips Trump administration at 2026 Harvard commencement
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien delivered a 25-minute, humor-filled commencement address at Harvard, addressing graduates for the third time.
O'Brien criticized the Trump administration's efforts to limit foreign student enrollment and referenced his own comedic career.
He urged graduates to embrace humility and community, contrasting current political climate focused on individualism.

title: Healey issues new ICE guidance for schools, hospitals, and places of worship
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey issued guidance for schools, hospitals, and worship places on interacting with ICE agents.
The guidance advises designated ICE contacts, protection of information, and requires judicial warrants for ICE entry.
Healey's executive order bans new 287(g) agreements, and prohibits ICE civil arrests in nonpublic state areas.

title: 3 flee after allegedly trying to climb iconic Boston weather beacon
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three teens reportedly attempted to climb the weather beacon atop the Old John Hancock Building in Boston.
Police were called to 200 Berkeley St. at 8:21 p.m. but found no suspects or forced entry on the rooftop.
The teens allegedly knocked a security guard to the ground and fled to Stuart Street; the investigation is ongoing.

title: 21-year-old motorcyclist allegedly went 139 mph in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: Johnathin Hutchins, 21, was arrested for allegedly driving 139 mph on a Suzuki motorcycle in Swanzey, NH.
The speeding incident occurred in a 50-mph zone on West Swanzey Road northbound on Tuesday evening.
Hutchins was charged with reckless driving and released on personal recognizance; court date set for July 1.
